zh.lifeSummary: 當Matilda FitzHubert de Rie於1070年出生在英國維爾爾索爾茲伯里時，她的父親Ranulph Fitz-Hubert de Rie25歲，母親Mathilda de Derbyshire20歲。她和Edward of Salisbury至少有1個兒子。她去世於1130年在英國英格蘭德比夏爾可利享年60歲，埋葬在英國英格蘭布里斯托。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Matilda FitzHubert de Rie于1070年出生在英国維爾爾索爾茲伯里时，她的父亲Ranulph Fitz-Hubert de Rie25岁，母亲Mathilda de Derbyshire20岁。她和Edward of Salisbury至少有1个儿子。她去世于1130年在英国英格兰德比夏爾可利享年60岁，埋葬在英国英格兰布里斯托。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Rie
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Scandinavian Aase, Lief, Sven.
Norwegian: habitational name from any of over twenty farmsteads named Rise, from Old Norse hrís ‘brushwood’. The name also occurs in Sweden and Denmark.

variants.name: Rice
meanings: Welsh: Anglicized pronunciation of one of the most common Welsh personal names, Rhys, from a form originally meaning ‘rash, impetuous’, also spelled Rys and Re(e)s. See also Reese , with which it is interchangeable as a result of different Anglicized forms of the Welsh vowel y, and also compare Preece and Price . Initial R- in Welsh is voiceless and often spelled Rh-, but in English R- is voiced as in the Anglicized surnames Rees and Rice. Welsh y is a short back vowel /ɪ/. In the medieval period the English approximation of this vowel was either /i/ or /e/, lengthened to /i:/ and /e:/. Subsequent sound changes in English produced the alternative pronunciations represented in Rees, Preece and Rice, Price. The name has also been established in Ireland from an early date.

English: either a topographic name for someone who lived in or near a thicket (Middle English ris, rice, ris, from Old English hrīs, Old Norse hrís), or a habitational name for someone who came from a place called with this word, such as Rise (East Yorkshire).

English: perhaps a nickname from Middle English Rys(e) and Re(e)s which when without a preposition could derive from one or other of several Old French and Middle English words, including Anglo-Norman French ris ‘laughter, smile’, Middle English ris, res ‘stem, stalk’, in origin the same word as in 2 above, and Middle English ris, rise, rice, res, Old French ris, riz ‘rice’, perhaps a nickname for a rice dealer or a cook.

nameMeanings.name: Matilda
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Latinized form of a Germanic personal name derived from maht, meht ‘might’ + hild ‘battle’. This was the name of an early German queen ( 895–968 ), wife of Henry the Fowler, who was noted for her piety and generosity. It was also the name of the wife of William the Conqueror and of the daughter of Henry I of England ( see Maud ). The name was introduced into England by the Normans, and this Latinized form is the one that normally occurs in medieval records, while the vernacular form Maud was the one in everyday use. Matilda was revived in England as a learned form in the 18th century.

variants.name: Mathilda
meanings: Latinized form of a Germanic personal name derived from maht, meht ‘might’ + hild ‘battle’. This was the name of an early German queen ( 895–968 ), wife of Henry the Fowler, who was noted for her piety and generosity. It was also the name of the wife of William the Conqueror and of the daughter of Henry I of England ( see Maud ). The name was introduced into England by the Normans, and this Latinized form is the one that normally occurs in medieval records, while the vernacular form Maud was the one in everyday use. Matilda was revived in England as a learned form in the 18th century.

historicEvents.title: Domesday Book
historicEvents.description: Domesday Book is Britain's earliest extant public record with information containing land surveys and landholdings commissioned by William I. The book was completed in 1086.

historicEvents.title: The Crusades
historicEvents.description: A series of religious wars between Christians and Muslims, known as the Crusades, occurred between 1096 and 1291, and played a large role in European Christian dominance in the Middle East.

historicEvents.title: The Battle of Tichebray
historicEvents.description: King Henry I defeated and captured his brother, Robert of Normandy, at the Battle of Tinchebray in 1106. Robert remained in captivity until his death in 1134.

en.lifeSummary: When Matilda FitzHubert de Rie was born in 1070, in Salisbury, Wiltshire, England, her father, Ranulph Fitz-Hubert de Rie, was 25 and her mother, Mathilda de Derbyshire, was 20. She had at least 1 son with Edward of Salisbury. She died in 1130, in Crich, Derbyshire, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 60, and was buried in Bristol, England, United Kingdom.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Matilda FitzHubert de Rie est née en 1070, à Salisbury, Wiltshire, Angleterre, son père, Ranulph Fitz-Hubert de Rie, avait 25 ans et sa mère, Mathilda de Derbyshire, avait 20 ans. Elle a eu au moins 1 fils avec Edward of Salisbury. Elle est décédée en 1130, à Crich, Derbyshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 60 ans, et a été enterrée à Bristol, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

de.lifeSummary: Als Matilda FitzHubert de Rie 1070 in Salisbury, Wiltshire, England geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Ranulph Fitz-Hubert de Rie 25 und ihre Mutter Mathilda de Derbyshire 20. Sie hatte mit Edward of Salisbury mindestens 1 Sohn. Sie starb 1130 im Alter von 60 Jahren in Crich, Derbyshire, England, United Kingdom und wurde in Bristol, England, United Kingdom begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Matilda FitzHubert de Rie è nata il nel 1070 nel Salisbury, Wiltshire, England, sua padre, Ranulph Fitz-Hubert de Rie, aveva 25 anni e sua madre, Mathilda de Derbyshire, aveva 20 anni. Lei avuto almeno 1 figlio con Edward of Salisbury. Morì nel 1130, nel Crich, Derbyshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, all'età di 60 anni, e fu sepolta a Bristol, Inghilterra, Regno Unito.

ja.lifeSummary: Matilda FitzHubert de Rieが1070年にイギリスウィルトシュアイアサリスブリーで生まれた時，父Ranulph Fitz-Hubert de Rieは25歳，母Mathilda de Derbyshireは20歳であった。彼女はEdward of Salisburyとの間に少なくとも1人の息子をもうけた。彼女は1130年にイギリスイギリスデルブイシュアイアクリチで60歳で死去し，イギリスイギリスブリストルにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Matilda FitzHubert de Rie(이)가 1070년 영국 윌츠히르 살리스버이에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Ranulph Fitz-Hubert de Rie(은)는 25세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Mathilda de Derbyshire(은)는 20세였다. 그녀는 Edward of Salisbury(와)과 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다. 그녀는 1130년 영국 잉글랜드 더비실르 크리치에서 60세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드 브리스톨에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Matilda FitzHubert de Rie nasceu em 1070, em Salisbury, Wiltshire, Inglaterra, seu pai, Ranulph Fitz-Hubert de Rie, tinha 25 anos e sua mãe, Mathilda de Derbyshire, tinha 20 anos. Ela teve pelo menos 1 filho com Edward of Salisbury. Ela faleceu em 1130, em Crich, Derbyshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, com 60 anos, e foi sepultada em Bristol, Inglaterra, United Kingdom.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Matilda FitzHubert de Rie родилась в 1070 г. в местности салисбури, уилцхир, Англия, её отцу по имени Ranulph Fitz-Hubert de Rie было 25 лет, а матери, которую звали Mathilda de Derbyshire, 20. У неё был по крайней мере 1 сын от Edward of Salisbury. Она умерла в 1130 г. в местности крич, дирбишир, Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 60 лет и была похоронена в местности бристол, Англия, Великобритания.
